Title: Lab order placed in CPOE propagates to lab result entry queue
Preconditions: Clinician logged in with active visit; lab order type configured; result entry user available
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Place a lab order (CBC) via Orders | 3. Save and sign the order | 4. Log in as lab tech | 5. Open the lab/result worklist
Expected_Result: Order appears in lab result worklist with matching patient, order number, accession and ordered datetime; status=Ordered/In-Progress

Title: Lab result entry back-propagates to ordering encounter and dashboard
Preconditions: Lab order from TC-INTEG-0001 in worklist
Test_Steps: 1. As lab tech enter numeric result for CBC components | 2. Save result obs | 3. Switch to clinician session | 4. Open patient dashboard | 5. View Results/Recent results widget
Expected_Result: Result obs linked to original order; visible on dashboard under the ordering encounter with correct datetime and ordering provider

Title: Completed lab order generates a billable line item
Preconditions: Billing module enabled; lab service mapped to a billable concept/price
Test_Steps: 1. Place and complete a billable lab order | 2. Mark result final | 3. Open patient billing/charges | 4. Inspect generated charge
Expected_Result: A charge line for the lab service appears with correct code, quantity=1 and unit price; charge references the order/encounter

Title: Cancelled order before fulfillment does not create a charge
Preconditions: Billing enabled; billable lab order placed but not fulfilled
Test_Steps: 1. Place a billable lab order | 2. Cancel/void the order prior to result | 3. Open billing/charges | 4. Verify no charge created
Expected_Result: No billable line item is created for the cancelled order; audit log records cancellation

Title: Charge propagation decision table by order status
Preconditions: Billing enabled; statuses configurable per order
Test_Steps: 1. For each order status, drive order to that state | 2. Inspect whether a charge is generated | 3. Record actual vs expected
Test_Data: Statuses: Ordered=no; In-Progress=no; Completed=yes; Cancelled=no; Voided=no; Discontinued=no
Expected_Result: Charge generated only for Completed/Final status; all non-final statuses produce no charge; matrix matches expected column

Title: Drug order propagates to pharmacy dispense queue
Preconditions: Pharmacy module enabled; clinician with prescribing rights; drug formulary configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Create a drug order (Amoxicillin 500mg TID) | 3. Sign order | 4. Log in as pharmacist | 5. Open dispensing worklist
Expected_Result: Drug order appears in pharmacy queue with correct drug, dose, frequency, route and prescriber; status=Active

Title: Allergy from clinical module blocks conflicting drug order at pharmacy handoff
Preconditions: Patient has documented penicillin allergy; interaction checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm penicillin allergy on patient | 2. Attempt to order Amoxicillin | 3. Observe interaction/allergy alert | 4. Verify pharmacy queue state
Expected_Result: Allergy alert is raised at ordering; if overridden, override reason is captured and propagated to pharmacy with warning flag

Title: Radiology order propagates to modality worklist and report back-flows
Preconditions: Radiology module/order type enabled; reporting radiologist available
Test_Steps: 1. Place a radiology order (Chest X-ray) | 2. Sign order | 3. As radiologist open imaging worklist | 4. Enter a report/impression | 5. Verify on patient dashboard
Expected_Result: Order appears in imaging worklist; entered report links back to the order and is visible on the patient dashboard

Title: Encounters created across modules link to the same active visit
Preconditions: Patient has one active visit; vitals, notes and orders configured
Test_Steps: 1. Start active visit | 2. Add vitals encounter | 3. Add a consultation note encounter | 4. Place an order (creates order encounter) | 5. Review visit summary
Expected_Result: All three encounters appear under the single active visit with the same visit id; no orphan encounters created

Title: Encounter created with no active visit auto-associates or is rejected per config
Preconditions: Auto-visit-assignment setting configurable
Test_Steps: 1. Ensure patient has no active visit | 2. Attempt to create an encounter | 3. Observe behavior for each config value | 4. Record outcome
Test_Data: autoVisit=on => encounter attaches to new/recent visit; autoVisit=off => encounter saved without visit or blocked
Expected_Result: Behavior matches configured policy deterministically; no silent data loss; encounter visit linkage is consistent with setting

Title: Checked-in appointment creates/links an active visit
Preconditions: Appointment scheduling enabled; scheduled appointment exists for today
Test_Steps: 1. Open today appointment for patient | 2. Mark patient Checked-In | 3. Verify an active visit is created or linked | 4. Confirm clinician can document on it
Expected_Result: Check-in transitions appointment status and surfaces an active visit; documentation can proceed against that visit

Title: Appointment lifecycle state transitions are consistent across modules
Preconditions: Appointment exists; statuses: Scheduled, CheckedIn, Completed, Missed, Cancelled
Test_Steps: 1. Drive appointment Scheduled->CheckedIn->Completed | 2. Attempt invalid transition Completed->CheckedIn | 3. Attempt Cancelled->Completed | 4. Record allowed vs blocked
Expected_Result: Valid transitions succeed and reflect in calendar and visit; invalid transitions are blocked with a clear error; state never corrupts

Title: New OpenMRS patient is retrievable via FHIR R4 Patient resource
Preconditions: FHIR2 module enabled; /ws/fhir2/R4 reachable; valid auth
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient in OpenMRS UI | 2. Note generated identifier | 3. GET /Patient?identifier=<id> via FHIR | 4. Validate resource fields
Expected_Result: FHIR Patient returned with matching name, gender, birthDate and identifier; resource validates against R4 Patient profile

Title: Vitals encounter surfaces as FHIR Observation resources
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; patient with a saved vitals encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Save vitals (BP, pulse, temp) | 2. GET /Observation?patient=<id>&category=vital-signs | 3. Inspect returned Observations
Expected_Result: Observations returned with correct LOINC codes, values, units (UCUM) and effectiveDateTime; linked to patient and encounter

Title: Create patient via FHIR POST appears in OpenMRS registry
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; auth user with create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST a valid R4 Patient bundle/resource to /Patient | 2. Capture returned id | 3. Search the patient in OpenMRS UI | 4. Verify demographics
Expected_Result: HTTP 201 with Location; patient is searchable in OpenMRS with matching demographics and FHIR-sourced identifier

Title: Reject malformed FHIR resource with OperationOutcome
Preconditions: FHIR2 endpoint reachable; valid auth
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Patient resource missing required fields/invalid datatype | 2. Inspect response status and body
Expected_Result: HTTP 4xx with OperationOutcome describing the validation error; no partial patient created in OpenMRS

Title: Conditional create via If-None-Exist prevents duplicate patient
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; patient with known identifier exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST Patient with header If-None-Exist=identifier=<existing> | 2. Observe response | 3. Confirm no duplicate created
Expected_Result: Server returns 200 referencing existing resource (or 412 per policy); no duplicate patient is created; behavior documented

Title: Inbound ADT^A01 admit message creates/updates patient and visit
Preconditions: HL7 listener/queue configured; sender allowed
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a valid ADT^A01 message to the inbound interface | 2. Allow processing | 3. Verify patient exists/updated | 4. Verify visit/admission created
Expected_Result: Message accepted (ACK AA); patient created/matched; admission visit recorded with correct location and datetime

Title: Inbound ORU^R01 result message records observations
Preconditions: HL7 listener configured; patient with matching MRN exists
Test_Steps: 1. Submit ORU^R01 with OBX result segments | 2. Allow processing | 3. View results on patient dashboard
Expected_Result: ACK AA returned; observations stored with correct value, units and observation datetime; linked to patient

Title: Malformed HL7 message is rejected with negative ACK and queued for review
Preconditions: HL7 listener configured; error/dead-letter queue available
Test_Steps: 1. Submit HL7 message with bad MSH/segment ordering | 2. Observe ACK | 3. Inspect error queue
Expected_Result: ACK AE/AR returned; message routed to error queue with reason; no partial clinical data persisted

Title: Unknown MRN in inbound result triggers patient-matching exception
Preconditions: HL7 listener configured; matching policy = no auto-create on result
Test_Steps: 1. Submit ORU^R01 with MRN not present | 2. Observe processing outcome
Expected_Result: Message is held/errored with unmatched-patient reason; no orphan observation created; alert raised for reconciliation

Title: Registration event emits outbound ADT^A04 to subscribed system
Preconditions: Outbound HL7 channel configured to a stub receiver
Test_Steps: 1. Register a new patient | 2. Capture outbound message at stub receiver | 3. Validate segments
Expected_Result: An ADT^A04 message is emitted with correct PID demographics and MRN; receiver returns ACK AA; emission logged

Title: Eligibility check succeeds against WireMock insurance stub
Preconditions: WireMock running with eligibility stub; payer connector configured to stub URL
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger insurance eligibility for a patient with coverage | 2. WireMock returns active coverage | 3. Verify result surfaced in billing/registration
Expected_Result: Connector parses stub response; coverage shows Active with plan details; result stored against patient coverage

Title: Eligibility returns inactive coverage and blocks billing path
Preconditions: WireMock stub configured to return inactive coverage
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger eligibility for member with inactive plan | 2. Stub returns coverage=inactive | 3. Verify downstream handling
Expected_Result: System marks coverage Inactive; self-pay/alternate path is offered; no claim auto-submitted on inactive coverage

Title: Eligibility connector handles 500 from payer stub gracefully
Preconditions: WireMock stub set to return HTTP 500 for eligibility
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger eligibility | 2. Stub returns 500 | 3. Observe connector behavior and user message
Expected_Result: Connector surfaces a transient error (not a crash); request is retried per policy or queued; user sees a non-PHI-leaking error

Title: Eligibility connector honors timeout when stub delays response
Preconditions: WireMock stub configured with fixed delay exceeding connector timeout
Test_Steps: 1. Set stub delay > configured timeout | 2. Trigger eligibility | 3. Measure connector behavior at boundary
Expected_Result: Connector aborts at timeout, returns timeout error and does not hang the UI thread; request logged for retry

Title: Outbound lab order reaches WireMock LIS stub with correct payload
Preconditions: WireMock LIS stub running; lab interface configured to stub
Test_Steps: 1. Place a lab order routed to external LIS | 2. Capture request at WireMock | 3. Validate payload fields
Expected_Result: WireMock receives the order with correct patient id, test code, priority and ordering provider; request matches stub mapping

Title: Asynchronous result callback from LIS stub updates the order
Preconditions: WireMock configured to POST result callback; order placed and pending
Test_Steps: 1. Place external lab order | 2. WireMock posts result callback for the order | 3. Verify order/result state updates
Expected_Result: Result callback is matched to the correct order via accession; order moves to Completed; result visible on dashboard

Title: ePrescribe message reaches WireMock pharmacy stub
Preconditions: WireMock pharmacy stub running; eRx connector configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create a prescription flagged for external pharmacy | 2. Send eRx | 3. Capture request at WireMock
Expected_Result: WireMock receives eRx with correct drug, sig, quantity, refills and prescriber NPI; acknowledgement processed

Title: Duplicate inbound message with same control id is processed once
Preconditions: Inbound interface with idempotency keyed on message control id
Test_Steps: 1. Submit an inbound message (HL7/FHIR) with control id X | 2. Resubmit identical message with same control id X | 3. Inspect resulting records
Expected_Result: Second submission is recognized as duplicate; exactly one clinical record results; ACK indicates duplicate handled

Title: Retried outbound webhook with same idempotency-key creates no duplicate downstream record
Preconditions: Outbound webhook sends Idempotency-Key header; stub records keys
Test_Steps: 1. Emit event triggering outbound webhook | 2. Force a retry of the same delivery | 3. Inspect stub for duplicate processing
Expected_Result: Stub treats retry as idempotent; downstream creates a single record; delivery marked succeeded once

Title: Same logical order submitted twice with different control ids is detected as duplicate by business key
Preconditions: Duplicate-detection on business key (patient+test+timestamp window) enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Submit order message control id A | 2. Submit identical clinical order content with control id B within window | 3. Inspect orders
Expected_Result: Business-level duplicate is flagged for review or suppressed per policy; clinician not exposed to two identical active orders

Title: Transient downstream failure triggers bounded exponential backoff retries
Preconditions: Outbound connector with retry policy; stub fails N times then succeeds
Test_Steps: 1. Configure stub to fail 2 times then 200 | 2. Trigger delivery | 3. Observe retry attempts and timing | 4. Confirm eventual success
Expected_Result: Connector retries with increasing backoff, succeeds on 3rd attempt; no duplicate downstream record; attempts logged

Title: Permanent failure after max retries routes message to dead-letter queue
Preconditions: Outbound connector with max-retry; stub always returns 500
Test_Steps: 1. Configure stub to always fail | 2. Trigger delivery | 3. Wait for retries to exhaust | 4. Inspect DLQ
Expected_Result: After max retries the message is moved to DLQ with failure reason and original payload; alert/metric raised; no silent drop

Title: Dead-letter message can be replayed after stub recovery without duplication
Preconditions: DLQ contains a failed delivery; idempotency keys preserved
Test_Steps: 1. Recover stub to 200 | 2. Replay the DLQ message | 3. Verify downstream record | 4. Verify message cleared from DLQ
Expected_Result: Replay delivers successfully; idempotency prevents duplicate downstream record; DLQ entry resolved and audited

Title: Patient demographic update emits an event consumed by subscribed modules
Preconditions: Event bus/subscription enabled; a subscriber stub listening
Test_Steps: 1. Update patient demographics (address) | 2. Capture emitted event at subscriber | 3. Validate event payload
Expected_Result: An update event is emitted with patient id and changed fields; subscriber receives it once; payload is PHI-minimized per policy

Title: Order-completed event triggers notification to ordering provider
Preconditions: Notification module enabled; provider has notification preference
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a lab order | 2. Confirm order-completed event fires | 3. Verify provider notification generated
Expected_Result: Event triggers a notification to the ordering provider with non-PHI-excessive content; notification logged and deduplicated

Title: Out-of-order event delivery does not corrupt final state
Preconditions: Event consumer must tolerate reordering; events carry version/sequence
Test_Steps: 1. Deliver order events out of sequence (Completed before In-Progress) | 2. Observe consumer state | 3. Verify final state
Expected_Result: Consumer uses version/sequence to converge to correct final state (Completed); no regression to stale state

Title: Registration to billing handoff carries guarantor and coverage data
Preconditions: Registration and billing modules enabled; coverage captured at registration
Test_Steps: 1. Register patient with insurance/guarantor | 2. Open billing for that patient | 3. Verify coverage and guarantor populated
Expected_Result: Billing reflects the coverage and guarantor captured at registration without re-entry; identifiers match

Title: Diagnosis captured in clinical note flows to encounter diagnosis and reporting
Preconditions: Clinical note with coded diagnosis; reporting module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Document a coded diagnosis (ICD-10) in a note | 2. Save encounter | 3. Verify it appears as encounter diagnosis | 4. Run a diagnosis report
Expected_Result: Coded diagnosis is stored at encounter level and is countable in the diagnosis report with correct code/display

Title: Pairwise coverage of order routing across type x destination x priority
Preconditions: Lab, radiology and pharmacy destinations configured; priorities supported
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination place an order | 2. Verify routing to correct destination queue | 3. Verify priority honored
Expected_Result: Each combination routes to the correct destination with correct priority; no cross-routing; pairwise matrix passes

Title: MedicationRequest reflects active drug order with correct dosage
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; patient with an active drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Create active drug order | 2. GET /MedicationRequest?patient=<id>&status=active | 3. Validate dosageInstruction
Expected_Result: MedicationRequest returned with status=active, correct medication code, dose, frequency and route mapped to FHIR

Title: DiagnosticReport links Observations to the originating ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR2 enabled; completed lab order with results
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a lab order with results | 2. GET /DiagnosticReport?patient=<id> | 3. Trace result and basedOn references
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport references the result Observations and basedOn the ServiceRequest; codes and effectiveDateTime correct

Title: Transaction bundle is atomic - one failed entry rolls back the whole bundle
Preconditions: FHIR2 supports transaction bundles
Test_Steps: 1. POST a transaction Bundle with a valid Patient and an invalid Observation | 2. Inspect response | 3. Verify no resources persisted
Test_Data: Bundle: Patient ok + Observation invalid code
Expected_Result: Whole transaction fails with OperationOutcome; neither Patient nor Observation is persisted; atomicity preserved

Title: Batch bundle processes entries independently with per-entry status
Preconditions: FHIR2 supports batch bundles
Test_Steps: 1. POST a batch Bundle with one valid and one invalid entry | 2. Inspect entry-level responses
Expected_Result: Valid entry succeeds (201) and invalid entry fails (4xx) independently; response Bundle reports per-entry status

Title: Inbound HL7 result is later retrievable via FHIR Observation
Preconditions: HL7 inbound and FHIR2 both enabled; matching patient
Test_Steps: 1. Submit ORU^R01 result via HL7 | 2. After processing GET /Observation?patient=<id> via FHIR
Expected_Result: The HL7-ingested result is exposed as a FHIR Observation with consistent value, units and datetime across interfaces

Title: Outbound interface payloads exclude unnecessary PHI per minimum-necessary
Preconditions: Outbound channel configured; PHI-minimization policy defined
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger outbound event/message | 2. Capture payload at stub | 3. Inspect for excessive PHI
Expected_Result: Payload contains only the fields required for the use case; SSN/full demographics not leaked beyond policy; verified at stub

Title: Inbound interface rejects requests with invalid or expired auth token
Preconditions: FHIR/REST inbound requires OAuth/basic auth
Test_Steps: 1. Send inbound request with expired/invalid token | 2. Observe response | 3. Verify no data processed
Expected_Result: HTTP 401/403; no clinical data created or read; failed attempt is audit-logged with source

Title: Integration endpoints enforce TLS and reject plaintext HTTP
Preconditions: Endpoints configured for HTTPS only
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to call inbound endpoint over plain HTTP | 2. Observe behavior
Expected_Result: Plaintext request is refused or redirected to HTTPS; no PHI is transmitted over an unencrypted channel

Title: Every inbound and outbound interface transaction is audit-logged
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; DB access for verification
Test_Steps: 1. Perform one inbound and one outbound transaction | 2. Query audit/integration log table | 3. Verify entries
Expected_Result: Both transactions produce audit records with timestamp, direction, message id, status and actor; no PHI in clear in logs beyond policy

Title: Order-result-billing foreign key integrity is preserved across handoff
Preconditions: DB read access; one completed billable order with result
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a billable order with result | 2. Query order, obs and charge tables | 3. Verify foreign keys
Expected_Result: Result obs references the order; charge references order/encounter; no dangling references; referential integrity intact

Title: Concurrent result writes for same order do not create duplicate observations
Preconditions: Ability to issue concurrent result submissions for one order
Test_Steps: 1. Submit two concurrent result writes for the same order/accession | 2. Inspect resulting observations
Test_Data: Two parallel ORU for accession ACC-9
Expected_Result: Exactly one canonical result set persists (or both flagged for reconciliation); no silent duplicate that misleads clinicians

Title: Maximum-size inbound message at the configured limit is accepted
Preconditions: Inbound size limit configured
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a message exactly at the max size limit | 2. Submit one byte over the limit | 3. Compare outcomes
Expected_Result: At-limit message is accepted and processed; over-limit message is rejected with a clear error; no truncation/corruption

Title: Batch of maximum supported entries in one FHIR bundle is handled
Preconditions: FHIR2 batch supported; max entries documented
Test_Steps: 1. POST a batch bundle at the max entry count | 2. POST one over the max | 3. Compare responses
Expected_Result: At-max bundle processes fully; over-max is rejected gracefully (413/OperationOutcome) without partial silent processing

Title: Explore race between visit close and late-arriving result
Preconditions: Ability to close a visit and then deliver a result for it
Test_Steps: 1. Place an order on an active visit | 2. Close/stop the visit | 3. Deliver the result after visit closure | 4. Observe linkage
Expected_Result: Late result still links to the originating order/encounter and is visible; it does not silently disappear or reopen visit improperly

Title: Explore behavior when external payer returns unexpected extra fields
Preconditions: WireMock payer stub returns valid response plus unknown JSON fields
Test_Steps: 1. Configure stub with extra/unknown fields | 2. Trigger eligibility | 3. Observe parsing robustness
Expected_Result: Connector ignores unknown fields gracefully and still parses known data; no crash; forward-compatible parsing

Title: Critical/panic lab value triggers high-priority alert across modules
Preconditions: Critical value thresholds configured; notification enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a result exceeding the critical threshold | 2. Confirm critical flag set | 3. Verify high-priority alert to provider
Expected_Result: Result is flagged critical; a high-priority alert is generated and delivered; alert acknowledgement is tracked/audited

Title: Notification suppressed when patient has opted out of that channel
Preconditions: Patient communication preferences captured; channels: SMS, email, none
Test_Steps: 1. Set patient preference per row | 2. Trigger a routine notification | 3. Verify delivery vs suppression
Expected_Result: Notifications honor per-channel preferences; opted-out channels are suppressed; critical safety alerts follow safety-override policy

Title: FHIR Subscription notifies subscriber when a matching resource is created
Preconditions: FHIR Subscription supported/configured; subscriber endpoint stub
Test_Steps: 1. Create a Subscription with a criteria (Observation?category=vital-signs) | 2. Create a matching Observation | 3. Verify notification at subscriber
Expected_Result: Subscriber receives a notification for the matching resource; non-matching resources do not trigger; per R4 subscription semantics

Title: Cross-system timestamps are stored with timezone and reconcile correctly
Preconditions: Inbound interfaces send timestamps with offsets; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Send an inbound observation with a non-UTC offset | 2. Inspect stored datetime | 3. Compare to displayed value
Expected_Result: Timestamp is stored unambiguously (UTC or with offset) and displayed correctly in local time; no off-by-timezone errors

Title: Adapter abstracts SUT so same integration test runs against OpenEMR target
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured with OpenEMR adapter; test harness portable
Test_Steps: 1. Point adapter to OpenEMR backend | 2. Run patient-create-then-FHIR-read flow | 3. Compare to OpenMRS baseline behavior
Expected_Result: Flow passes via the adapter with target-specific mappings; functional outcome matches the OpenMRS baseline contract

Title: Adapter maps code systems consistently across SUTs
Preconditions: Adapter code-system mapping table defined for each target
Test_Steps: 1. For each target adapter, send a coded value | 2. Verify mapped code system/URI | 3. Record matrix
Expected_Result: Each adapter emits the correct code system URI per target; no mismatched or missing system; mapping matrix passes

Title: SMART app launch obtains scoped token and reads only authorized resources
Preconditions: SMART on FHIR authorization server configured; app registered with scopes
Test_Steps: 1. Launch SMART app and complete auth | 2. Request a resource within granted scope | 3. Request a resource outside scope
Expected_Result: In-scope read succeeds; out-of-scope read returns 403; token scopes are enforced; launch context patient honored

Title: Partial multi-step E2E failure leaves no half-completed clinical state
Preconditions: Order-result-billing E2E flow; ability to inject failure mid-flow
Test_Steps: 1. Start order->result->charge flow | 2. Inject failure after result but before charge commit | 3. Inspect all three records
Expected_Result: No inconsistent state: either compensating action reverses prior steps or pending step is retried; clinician sees coherent status

Title: Integration error and reconciliation screens are accessible
Preconditions: Integration admin/error-queue UI available; screen reader and keyboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open the integration error/DLQ screen | 2. Navigate via keyboard only | 3. Verify error rows and actions announced by screen reader
Expected_Result: All controls are keyboard-reachable with visible focus; error rows and statuses are announced; WCAG 2.1 AA met for the screen

Title: Encounter void propagates retraction to downstream consumers
Preconditions: Event-driven downstream subscriber; encounter previously emitted
Test_Steps: 1. Create and emit an encounter event | 2. Void the encounter | 3. Verify a retraction/void event is emitted and consumed
Expected_Result: A void/retraction event is emitted referencing the original; subscriber retracts/marks-void the downstream record; no stale data persists

Title: Claim submission to payer stub returns acceptance with claim id
Preconditions: WireMock payer claims stub running; billable encounter ready
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a claim for a billable encounter | 2. Stub returns 202/accepted with claim id | 3. Verify claim status stored
Expected_Result: Claim is submitted with correct member, codes and amount; payer claim id is stored against the encounter; status=Submitted

Title: Replayed payment posting does not double-credit the patient ledger
Preconditions: Payment posting carries idempotency key; ledger readable
Test_Steps: 1. Post a payment with key P-1 | 2. Replay the same posting with key P-1 | 3. Inspect patient ledger balance
Expected_Result: Replay is recognized as duplicate; ledger is credited exactly once; balance correct; no double-credit
